steps.title: Identify the appositive relationship
steps.content: The blank separates “French portrait artist” from “Marie-Denise Villers (1774–1821).” The second noun phrase is an appositive—it renames or identifies the artist.

steps.title: Apply the appositive punctuation rule
steps.content: Here “Marie-Denise Villers (1774–1821)” functions as an essential (restrictive) appositive identifying the previously unidentified “little-known French portrait artist,” so no punctuation is required between “artist” and the name. Choices A (em dash), C (colon), and D (comma) all signal nonessential phrasing, which doesn’t suit a restrictive appositive. That matches choice B — B is correct.
